data.title: December saw a 7.1% rise in HK mortgage approvals despite lower applications. HKMA approves HK$31.2B mortgages, hinting at property market revival.
data.description: In December 2025, Hong Kong Monetary Authority (HKMA) reported approving HK$31.2 billion in mortgages. This comes as a positive indicator for Hong Kong's property market, which had been experiencing a four-year slump. The approved mortgage amount signifies a potential revival in the property sector, hinting at increasing confidence among buyers and investors. The turnaround in the market demonstrates a promising shift and a potential economic recovery for Hong Kong.

data.title: XRP expected to hit $2.60 by year-end despite current bearish trend, with RSI at 33.20. Analysts project $2.60-$4.00 by 2026.
data.description: XRP is currently experiencing a period of consolidation around $1.75, with the Relative Strength Index (RSI) showing oversold conditions at 33.20. Analysts are anticipating a potential recovery towards the range of $2.60 to $4.00 by the end of 2026, citing a technical reset in the market.

data.title: $400M in cryptocurrency forfeited to DOJ in Helix Bitcoin mixer case, closing a probe into its 2014-2017 operations.
data.description: In a recent development, a conclusive court ruling has resulted in the transfer of ownership of seized cryptocurrency and assets to the United States government. This decision marks the culmination of a case linked to Helix’s activities during the period spanning from 2014 to 2017. The transfer encompasses a substantial value of cryptocurrency and assets being handed over to the US authorities, solidifying the outcome of the legal proceedings.

data.title: BNB price aims $950-$1,050 by Feb 2026 amidst current bearish trend. Binance Coin at $841 with neutral RSI & bearish MACD. Analysts optimistic.
data.description: The current trading price of Binance Coin (BNB) stands at $841, showing neutral Relative Strength Index (RSI) and bearish Moving Average Convergence Divergence (MACD) signals. Analysts predict a price range of $950 to $1,050 for BNB by February 2026, despite facing some short-term challenges.

data.title: Colgate-Palmolive's Q2 earnings outlook: 91 cents/share, revenue $5.12 billion, net sales up 2%. Shares closed at $85.24.
data.description: Colgate-Palmolive Company, listed on NYSE as CL, is set to announce its second-quarter earnings before markets open on Friday, January 30. Analysts are predicting a quarterly profit of 91 cents per share, matching the earnings from the same period last year. Revenue for the quarter is anticipated to be around $5.12 billion, compared to $4.94 billion in the previous year. In the third quarter, the company surpassed expectations with adjusted earnings per share at 91 cents, exceeding the estimated 89 cents. Quarterly sales stood at $5.131 billion, meeting market expectations. The net sales for the period saw a 2% increase. On the day before the announcement, Colgate-Palmolive shares closed at $85.24, marking a 0.3% rise. More details are available on the Benzinga Pro platform, including analyst ratings and stock information.

data.title: Panama Court voids Hong Kong firm's canal deal. Cheers to Trump’s plan for US control. CK Hutchison faces setback.
data.description: The recent ruling marks a success for President Trump, as he expressed his desire for American control of the canal. Conversely, it represents a setback for CK Hutchison, the ports operator with a longstanding presence in the area.

data.title: CIFF Guangzhou 2026 focuses on sustainable workspaces in Office and Commercial Space Exhibition from March 18-31.
data.description: The 57th edition of the China International Furniture Fair (CIFF Guangzhou 2026) is set to occur in Guangzhou from March 18 to 31 with the theme "CONNECT•CREATE." The Office and Commercial Space Exhibition, a key component of the Fair, will be showcased during this event.

data.title: Apple's China revenue up 38% in Q1 2026, iPhone 17 sales boost. Cook calls it 'Best iPhone Quarter in History.'
data.description: Apple Inc. (NASDAQ: AAPL) announced a remarkable 38% increase in revenue from Greater China during the first quarter of fiscal 2026, driven by exceptional iPhone sales and high customer engagement. CEO Tim Cook stated that this quarter had been the best in terms of iPhone sales in Greater China's history, largely due to the enthusiastic reception of the iPhone 17 series. Store traffic in China also saw a robust double-digit growth compared to the previous year, indicating a resurgence in a market that had recently caused concern amongst investors. Cook expressed satisfaction with the results, highlighting that the installed base in both Greater China and mainland China had reached an all-time high. The company experienced record numbers of customers upgrading their devices, and a significant increase in users switching from other platforms. Information from Worldpanel showed the positive trend in iPhone adoption.

data.title: Ethereum aims to rebound to $3,200-$3,400 by Feb 2026. Facing oversold conditions at $2,734 with RSI 34.69. Support crucial above $2,525.
data.description: Ethereum is currently experiencing technical oversold conditions at a price of $2,734, with the Relative Strength Index (RSI) at 34.69. According to analysis, there is a possibility of a recovery to the range of $3,200-$3,400 in February if crucial support levels remain above $2,525.

data.title: HSM market to reach $3.51Bn by 2031, growing at 10% CAGR due to increasing adoption of hardware security models and data protection mandates.
data.description: In Hyderabad, India, on January 30, 2026, a report from Mordor Intelligence revealed that the market for hardware security modules (HSM) is rapidly growing. The surge in demand is driven by enterprises focusing on enhancing cryptographic security, safeguarding data, and ensuring adherence to regulatory standards within their digital environments. With the HSM market witnessing significant expansion, this trend underscores the increasing importance placed on cybersecurity measures.

data.title: Wall Street analysts adjust Charter Communications' Q4 expectations. Company expected to report earnings of $9.88 per share, down from $10.1 in the year-ago period. Revenue estimate: $13.73 billion. Charter issued $3.0 billion in unsecured notes. Stock rose 4.8% to $191.52.
data.description: On Friday, January 30, Charter Communications, Inc. (NASDAQ: CHTR) is set to announce its fourth-quarter earnings. Analysts predict earnings of $9.88 per share, a decrease from $10.1 per share in the same period last year. The consensus estimate for quarterly revenue is $13.73 billion, slightly lower than the previous year’s reported $13.93 billion. Recently, the company finalized $3.0 billion in senior unsecured notes. Closing at $191.52 on Thursday, Charter Communications' stock rose by 4.8%. For more detailed information and analyst ratings, visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga.
